questions.question: In GPR surveys, how does the time delay between the transmitted pulse and the received signal relate to the depth of the reflecting interface?

answers.a: Time delay is inversely proportional to depth.
answers.b: Time delay is directly proportional to depth.
answers.c: Time delay is not related to depth.
answers.d: Time delay is logarithmically related to depth.

explanations.a: With increase of depth time delay decreases.
explanations.b: With increase in depth time delay increases.
explanations.d: Time delay and depth varies in logarithmic scale.

questions.question: Describe the role of Relative Dielectric Permittivity (RDP) in GPR surveys and how it affects the interpretation of subsurface structures.

answers.a: RDP influences the speed of the pulse, affecting depth estimation.
answers.b: RDP has no impact on GPR survey outcomes.
answers.c: RDP influences the amplitude of reflections, affecting clarity.
answers.d: RDP determines the color representation in GPR images.

explanations.a: In GPR, RDP impacts the pulse speed, consequently influencing the estimation of depth.
explanations.b: RDP does not impact GPR survey outcomes.
explanations.c: RDP affects the amplitude of reflections, influencing clarity in GPR images.
explanations.d: RDP determines the color representation in GPR images.

questions.question: In GPR data processing, what considerations are involved in selecting the optimal gain settings for a survey?

answers.d: Ignoring variations in the collected data.

explanations.a: Prioritizing the reduction of signal-to-noise ratio in geophysical surveys.
explanations.b: Emphasizing the improvement of subsurface feature visibility.
explanations.c: Avoiding the maximization of background noise in collected data.
explanations.d: Acknowledging and considering variations in the collected data during analysis.

questions.question: What type of material produces strong reflections in GPR due to its low electrical conductivity?

explanations.d: Solid mineral material forming the Earth's crust.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of GPR technology in subsurface investigations?

explanations.a: Imaging technique without causing harm to the object.
explanations.b: Enhancing sound waves for various applications.
explanations.c: Identifying and measuring gravitational waves in space.
explanations.d: Ability to capture and display infrared radiation to visualize temperature differences.
